Meta.Title: 3-day travel plan for Toronto
Meta.Summary: A compact 3-day itinerary covering downtown highlights, neighborhoods, museums, and a day trip option, with flexible meal and transit suggestions.

Morning.Item: Breakfast near Yonge-Dundas Square, then walk through the Eaton Centre area.
Visit St. Lawrence Market (browse vendors, snacks, quick lunch planning).

Afternoon.Item: Explore the Distillery Historic District for architecture, shops, and galleries.
Walk or transit to the Toronto waterfront; stroll Harbourfront Centre.

Evening.Item: CN Tower at sunset (consider EdgeWalk if desired); dinner nearby.

Notes.Item: Use TTC subway/streetcars for quick hops; consider a day pass if doing many short rides.

Morning.Item: Chinese food stop (dim sum or noodles) for a broad, easy half-day.
Walk through Yorkville for cafes and window-shopping.

Afternoon.Item: Casa Loma (tour and gardens); continue to nearby midtown streets for a relaxed walk.

Evening.Item: Dinner and exploring in Kensington Market and Chinatown (casual, varied options).
Optional: Live show in the Entertainment District (theater, comedy, or music).

Day.title: Parks, neighborhoods, and a day trip option

Morning.Item: Walk the Martin Goodman Trail or visit High Park (especially nice in spring/summer).
Brunch in Queen West or Ossington area.

Afternoon.Item: Option A (in-city): Toronto Islands ferry for skyline views, biking, and beaches.
Option B (day trip): Niagara Falls (GO Transit + local transit/tour) for a full afternoon.

Evening.Item: Return for a final dinner in King West, Queen West, or the waterfront.
Optional: Night views from Riverdale Park East or Polson Pier (weather permitting).

Notes.Item: If choosing Niagara Falls, start early and expect a longer day; otherwise prioritize the Islands.

Morning.Item: Breakfast in Queen West, then browse vinyl and music shops along Queen St W (record stores and indie boutiques).
Walk through Kensington Market for street art and busker vibes (best late morning).

Afternoon.Item: Pick a neighborhood music crawl: Ossington and Dundas West for indie venues, or Danforth for smaller stages and pubs.
Take a break at a cafe and build the evening plan: choose one marquee show or two smaller sets.

Evening.Item: Live music night: book tickets in advance for a concert, jazz set, or DJ night (venues in Queen West, King West, or the Danforth area).
Late-night bite nearby; if energy allows, finish with a second shorter set at a nearby bar venue.

Notes.Item: Check venue calendars a week or two ahead and align neighborhoods to minimize transit time.
If you want a quieter option, swap the crawl for a relaxed piano/jazz lounge and an earlier dinner.

Day.title: Flexible final day: neighborhoods, views, and food

Morning.Item: Slow breakfast/brunch in your favorite area from earlier days (Queen West, Ossington, or Kensington Market).
Light walk: choose one easy loop (Harbourfront and the waterfront trail, or the University of Toronto campus area).

Afternoon.Item: Pick one neighborhood you did not get to: Leslieville (cafes and shops) or the Junction (indie stores and breweries).
Optional downtime: relax in a park (Trinity Bellwoods or Riverdale Park) and leave space for shopping or rest.

Evening.Item: Farewell dinner in a food-heavy area (Kensington/Chinatown, King West, or Danforth) based on what you are craving.
Optional: casual nightcap or dessert, then an easy walk for skyline views if weather is good.

Notes.Item: Keep this day intentionally flexible for weather, energy, and anything you want to revisit.
If you have an early departure, shift the neighborhood pick to the morning and keep the evening simple.
